526.task: Write a function `wordWrap(text, maxWidth)` that wraps text into lines no longer than `maxWidth`. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

527.task: Write a function `escapeHtml(input)` that escapes HTML-sensitive characters. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

528.task: Write a function `unescapeHtml(input)` that decodes common HTML entities back into characters. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

529.task: Write a function `buildTree(items)` that converts a flat list of `{ id, parentId }` nodes into a tree. Then write tests for at least 10 cases.

530.task: Write a function `lruCache(capacity)` that returns an object with `get` and `put` methods implementing an LRU cache. Then write tests for at least 9 cases.

531.task: Write a function `parseDateISO(input)` that parses dates in strict `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Then write tests for at least 8 cases including invalid months, invalid days, leap year, non-leap year, empty, and null.

532.task: Write a function `relativePath(from, to)` that computes the relative filesystem path from one absolute path to another. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

533.task: Write a function `sortByMultiple(items, rules)` that sorts objects by multiple keys and directions. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

534.task: Write a function `parseBoolean(input)` that converts strings and primitives into boolean values using strict rules. Then write tests for at least 10 cases.

535.task: Write a function `transposeCsv(rows)` that accepts an array of CSV rows represented as arrays of strings and returns the transposed table. Then write tests for at least 6 cases.

536.task: Write a function `minWindowSubstring(text, pattern)` that returns the smallest substring of `text` containing all characters from `pattern` with multiplicity. Then write tests for at least 7 cases.

537.task: Write a function `detectCycle(head)` for a singly linked list that returns whether the list contains a cycle. Then write tests that build linked lists manually.

538.task: Write a function `serializeForm(fields)` that converts an array of `{ name, value }` pairs into a query string. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

539.task: Write a function `descriptiveStats(numbers)` that returns count, min, max, mean, median, mode, and range. Then write tests for at least 10 cases.

540.task: Write a function `validatePassword(password)` that checks a password against rules: minimum length 10, at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase, one digit, one special character. Then write tests for all rules.

541.task: Write a function `nearestPalindrome(n)` that returns the numerically closest palindrome to a positive integer string. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

542.task: Write a function `removeComments(code)` that removes `//` and `/* ... */` comments from a source string while preserving content inside quoted strings. Then write tests for at least 7 cases.

543.task: Write a function `evaluateRpn(tokens)` that evaluates an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation. Then write tests for at least 9 cases.

544.task: Write a function `findPeakElement(nums)` that returns the index of any peak element in an array. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

545.task: Write a function `searchRotatedArray(nums, target)` that finds `target` in a rotated sorted array and returns its index or `-1`. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

546.task: Write a function `romanToInt(input)` that converts a Roman numeral to an integer. Then write tests for at least 10 cases.

547.task: Write a function `intToRoman(input)` that converts an integer to a Roman numeral. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

548.task: Write a function `knapsack01(items, capacity)` that returns the maximum achievable value for the 0/1 knapsack problem. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

549.task: Write a function `longestCommonPrefix(words)` that returns the longest shared prefix among strings. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

550.task: Write a function `balancedBrackets(input)` that returns whether all brackets in a string are balanced for `()`, `[]`, and `{}` while ignoring non-bracket characters. Then write tests for at least 8 cases.

551.task: Explain how binary search works and why it requires sorted input, then write a function that returns the index of a target value in a sorted integer array or if not found.

552.task: Explain the difference between a stack and a queue, then write two classes implementing each data structure with push/pop and enqueue/dequeue operations.

553.task: Explain how hash maps achieve average-case constant-time lookup, then write a function that counts the frequency of each word in a string and returns a map of word to count.

554.task: Explain how recursion works and what a base case is, then write a recursive function that computes the factorial of a non-negative integer.

555.task: Explain memoization and how it improves recursive algorithms, then write a memoized function that returns the nth Fibonacci number.

556.task: Explain the two-pointer technique, then write a function that determines whether a sorted array contains two numbers whose sum equals a given target.

557.task: Explain sliding window algorithms, then write a function that returns the length of the longest substring of a string that contains no repeated characters.

558.task: Explain how depth-first search traverses a graph, then write a function that determines whether a path exists between two nodes in a directed graph.

559.task: Explain how breadth-first search differs from depth-first search, then write a function that returns the shortest path length between two nodes in an unweighted graph.

560.task: Explain topological sorting and when it is possible, then write a function that returns a valid topological ordering of a directed acyclic graph.

561.task: Explain how cycle detection works in graphs, then write a function that returns whether a directed graph contains a cycle.

562.task: Explain what a binary tree traversal is, then write a function that returns the inorder traversal of a binary tree as an array.

563.task: Explain the binary search tree property, then write a function that inserts a value into a binary search tree and returns the root.

564.task: Explain how heaps support priority queues, then write a min-heap class with insert and extractMin methods.

565.task: Explain merge sort and why its time complexity is O(n log n), then write a function that sorts an array of integers using merge sort.

566.task: Explain quicksort partitioning, then write a function that sorts an array of integers using quicksort.

567.task: Explain dynamic programming using subproblems and overlapping work, then write a function that computes the minimum number of coins needed to make a target amount from given coin denominations.

568.task: Explain the longest common subsequence problem, then write a function that returns the length of the longest common subsequence of two strings.

569.task: Explain edit distance and the allowed operations, then write a function that computes the Levenshtein distance between two strings.

570.task: Explain greedy algorithms and when they are valid, then write a function that schedules the maximum number of non-overlapping intervals from a list of start/end times.

571.task: Explain prefix sums, then write a function that answers multiple range-sum queries on an integer array in constant time per query after preprocessing.

572.task: Explain difference arrays, then write a function that applies a list of inclusive range increment operations to an array and returns the final array.

573.task: Explain union-find (disjoint set union), then write a class supporting find and union, and use it to count the number of connected components in an undirected graph.

574.task: Explain Dijkstra’s algorithm and its assumptions, then write a function that returns the shortest path distances from a source node in a graph with non-negative edge weights.

575.task: Explain why Bellman-Ford can handle negative edges, then write a function that computes shortest path distances from a source and detects whether a negative cycle is reachable.

576.task: Explain what a trie is and what problems it solves, then write a trie class supporting insert, search, and startsWith.

577.task: Explain backtracking, then write a function that returns all valid combinations of n pairs of balanced parentheses.

578.task: Explain permutation generation with backtracking, then write a function that returns all permutations of a list of distinct integers.

579.task: Explain how subset generation works, then write a function that returns the power set of a list of unique integers.

580.task: Explain the concept of stable sorting, then write a stable sort function for an array of objects by a specified numeric key.

581.task: Explain how regular expressions match patterns, then write a function that validates whether a string is a valid IPv4 address.

582.task: Explain string tokenization, then write a function that parses a CSV line into fields, correctly handling quoted commas and escaped quotes.

583.task: Explain URL query strings and percent-encoding, then write a function that parses a query string into a dictionary of key-value pairs.

584.task: Explain JSON serialization and parsing, then write a function that safely pretty-prints a JSON string with indentation or returns an error for invalid input.

585.task: Explain how timestamps and time zones differ, then write a function that converts a Unix timestamp in seconds into an ISO 8601 UTC string.

586.task: Explain the producer-consumer problem, then write thread-safe code using a blocking queue to allow one producer and one consumer to exchange integer messages.

587.task: Explain race conditions, then write code that safely increments a shared counter from multiple threads and returns the correct final value.

588.task: Explain asynchronous programming with promises or futures, then write a function that runs three asynchronous tasks in parallel and returns all results in original order.

589.task: Explain debounce behavior in event-driven systems, then write a debounced wrapper function that delays invoking a callback until no calls have occurred for 300 ms.

590.task: Explain throttle behavior and how it differs from debounce, then write a throttled wrapper function that ensures a callback runs at most once per 200 ms.

591.task: Explain least-recently-used caching, then write an LRUCache class with get and put operations in O(1) average time.

592.task: Explain how pagination works in APIs, then write a function that fetches all pages from a paginated endpoint that returns items and nextPageToken.

593.task: Explain REST idempotency, then write an HTTP handler for PUT /users/:id that replaces a user record with the request body.

594.task: Explain how JWT structure is organized into header, payload, and signature, then write a function that base64url-decodes the header and payload of a JWT without verifying the signature.

595.task: Explain password hashing and why salts are necessary, then write code that hashes a password with a random salt and verifies a login attempt against the stored hash.

596.task: Explain SQL parameterization and how it prevents injection, then write a function that queries a seifbenayed table by email using a parameterized statement.

597.task: Explain the difference between encryption and hashing, then write code that encrypts a plaintext string with AES and decrypts it back to the original value.

598.task: Explain rate limiting with a token bucket, then write a TokenBucket class that decides whether each incoming request should be allowed.

599.task: Explain how finite state machines model parsing, then write a function that validates whether a string is a valid decimal number with optional sign and fractional part.

600.task: Explain how markdown headings are structured, then write a function that parses a markdown document string and returns a nested table of contents based on heading levels.
